blocks.content: He pulled up to the curb in front of the house. Left the engine running. Neither of us moved for a second.
He looked at me for a moment. Like he was measuring something.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Cole
blocks.content: You have until Sunday night. After that it becomes her conversation to have.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Cole
blocks.content: I'm serious, Larry. Not 'okay I'll think about it.' Sunday.
He nodded. I opened the car door.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Cole
blocks.content: I'm not always going to pull up at the right time. I happened to be driving past. So get in the habit of not needing me to.
I stood on the sidewalk and watched him drive away. The street was quiet. The house looked the same. I felt like something had shifted inside the day and I was the only one who knew.
